title: Macroeconomic Nowcasting and Forecasting with Big Data

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: This is a 2017 Federal Reserve Bank of New York Staff Report describing the methodology behind the NY Fed Staff Nowcast, which synthesizes macroeconomic data to produce early estimates of GDP growth. The report reviews how econometric techniques have evolved to handle large datasets for real-time macroeconomic monitoring. The source provides only introductory metadata; full content is PDF-only and not accessible in this crawl.
key_points: Authors: Brandyn Bok, Daniele Caratelli, Domenico Giannone, Argia M. Sbordone, and Andrea Tambalotti
Published November 2017 as Staff Report Number 830
JEL classification: C32, C53, C55, E32
Report describes methodology for the NY Fed Staff Nowcast system
Techniques described as mimicking practices used on trading desks and at central banks
Full report available only in PDF format; crawl contains only page metadata
why_it_matters: This research paper documents the methodological foundation for the NY Fed's real-time GDP nowcasting system, which is a key input for monetary policy analysis. However, this source is from November 2017 (over 8 years old) and provides only metadata rather than full content.
macro_context: The report reviews methods for tracking economic conditions using big data, which inform real-time macroeconomic monitoring and policy decisions. As a 2017 methodological paper, it does not contain current market-relevant data or forecasts.
